title: Add custom date range selection to PDF Report page

requirements.summary: Add 'Custom Range' period selection mode to RapportPDFPage with start and end date pickers, reusing the existing table format for report generation
acceptanceCriteria: The report period selector on RapportPDFPage shows three options: By Month, By Year, and Custom Range.
When 'Custom Range' is selected, two date pickers appear: one for start date and one for end date.
The user can set start date to January 1st and end date to March 31st and generate a report covering exactly that period.
The generated report uses the same table format as the existing monthly/yearly reports.
Appointments and financial data are filtered to include only records whose dates fall within the selected start and end dates (inclusive).
The existing 'By Month' and 'By Year' modes continue to work as before without any regression.

file_operations.description: Add a third radio option 'Custom Range' to the period selector alongside 'By Month' and 'By Year'. Add state for start date and end date inputs. Conditionally render two date input fields when 'Custom Range' is selected. Wire the custom range inputs to trigger report generation using the same data aggregation logic as existing modes.

requirements.summary: Update data aggregation logic in RapportPDFPage to support filtering appointments by arbitrary date range, feeding into the same report-building pipeline
acceptanceCriteria: A shared filtering utility or inline logic accepts a startDate and endDate and returns appointments within that range.
The custom range report totals (revenue, appointments, credits) are computed correctly using the same calculation functions as the existing modes.
The PDF download triggered from custom range mode produces a valid, well-formatted document matching the existing PDF layout.

file_operations.description: Extend the data aggregation function to accept start and end timestamps for custom range filtering. Filter fetched appointments by checking if their dateHeure falls within the custom range (inclusive). Apply the same revenue, appointment count, and credit calculations as used in monthly and yearly modes. Ensure the PDF HTML generation logic correctly formats the custom range period label and reuses the existing table structure.
